The Melastomataceae family is part of the larger order Myrtales, which includes several other families of flowering plants. The order Myrtales is part of the clade Rosids, a large group of flowering plants that are often characterized by their flowers having five petals. Anerincleistus hispidissimus is a unique species within the broader classification of the Rosids clade.

This plant has been traditionally used in medicine and is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate conditions such as fever and infections.
